LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING RE: CROSS-COMPLAINANT DANIEL COWAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-COMPLAINANT DANIEL COWAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. NO TIMELY OPPOSITION FILED. THE COURT INSTRUCTS COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY TO EMAIL A PROPOSED ORDER AFTER HEARING TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO, CSR#8600, E-MAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS) *********** SEE SEP-20-2019 EX PARTE ORDER **********

LAW AND MOTION 302,HEARING ON PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO VACATE. CONTINUED TO 9/19/19 AT 9:30 AM. THE MOTION WILL BE HEARD AFTER THE PARTY'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL IS HEARD IN DEPT. 206. THE COURT IS INCLINED TO GRANT THE 473(B) MOTION AND TO FIND THAT THE ORDER GRANTING SUMMARY JUDGMNT WAS ENTERED AS A RESULT OF COUNSEL'S EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. PARTIES TO ADVISE THE PRESIDING JUDGE OF JUDGE SCHULMAN'S INTENDED RULING. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO BRING A PROPOSED OREDER ON 9/19/19. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES/TRANSCRIPT) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Judge: GARRETT L. WONG; Clerk: RAYMOND K. WONG; Proceedings not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-23-2019 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Nov-19-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Hearing On Plaintiff'S Motion To Vacate
Off Calendar Sep-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joseph Salama, Esq. 415-948-9030 for Plaintiff.
V. Sathienmars, Esq. 510-254-6763 of Kronenberg Law for Defendant Daniel Cowan et al.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff's Counsel reports the motion heard in Dept. 206 was Denied and the Current trial date is set for 9/23/19. The Court shares its views. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion To Vacate the Order on the Motion for Summary Judgment.

Granted. The Court sets aside the summary judgment order that was previously entered (heard on 8/26/19)under CCP 473 for excusable neglect by counsel.

Plaintiff's Counsel to prepare a proposed order setting aside the summary judgment order under 473(b) for excusable neglect by Counsel based on the prior hearing and motion.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Motion To Continue Trial And Reset All Deadlines In Accordance With New Date, Memoranum In Support; Declaration
Off Calendar Sep-19-2019 Text Ruling

Hearing On Plaintiff'S Motion To Vacate
Off Calendar Sep-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joseph Salama, Esq. 415-948-9030 for Plaintiff.
V. Sathienmars, Esq. 510-254-6763 of Kronenberg Law for Defendant Daniel Cowan.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. Plaintiff informs the Court of a motion to continue trial on 9/19/19 in Dept. 206.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Hearing On Plaintiff'S Motion To Vacate.

Continued to 9/19/19 at 9:30 am. The motion will be heard after the party's motion to Continue trial is heard in Dept. 206. The Court is inclined to grant the 473(b) motion and to find that the order granting summary judgmnt was entered as a result of Counsel's excusable neglect. Parties to advise the Presiding Judge of Judge Schulman's intended ruling. Plaintiff's Counsel to bring a proposed order on 9/19/19. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. JOSEPH SALAMA, ESQ. of Law Office of Joseph Salama (Tel: 415-948-9030) appearing for plaintiff/cross-defendant Gustavo Mendoza;
Atty. V. SATHIENMARS, ESQ. of Kronenberg Law (Tel: 510-254-6763) appearing for defendant/cross-complainant Daniel Cowan;

Hearing On Plaintiff's Motion To Vacate (Set Per Ex Parte Order Filed Sep-04-2019).

Matter argued by counsel. Counsel confer in the hallway as to possible agreements by the parties. Counsel update the Court. At counsel's request, the Court continues plaintiff's motion to vacate for further hearing to September 16, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion.

Motion To Set Aside Order On Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-12-2019 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of The Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. JOSEPH SALAMA, ESQ. of the Law Offices of Joseph Salama (Tel: 415-948-9030) appearing for Gustavo Mendoza;
Atty. MICHAEL KVYAT, ESQ. of Kronenberg Law (Tel: 510-254-6768) appearing for Daniel Cowan and UNX Enterprises LLC.

HEARING RE: CROSS-COMPLAINANT DANIEL COWAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of The Cross-Complaint.

The Court addresses counsel for Gustavo Mendoza's untimely intent to oppose. The Court indicates that counsel for Gustavo Mendoza would have to bring a motion under C.C.P. Sec. 473. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS-COMPLAINANT DANIEL COWAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Of The Cross-Complaint is Granted. No timely opposition filed.

The Court instructs counsel for the prevailing party to email a proposed order after hearing to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
